# Bella Illingworth — Personal Landing Page

*Replace with your project name if reusing this template.*

## Description

*2–4 sentences describing what the project does, why it exists, and who it is for.*

A single-page personal landing page built for Bella Illingworth, a Business Analytics and Information Systems student at the University of Iowa graduating in May 2026. The page provides recruiters, hiring managers, and product or analytics professionals with a clear, scannable summary of her background, work experience, skills, and featured projects. It was built as part of BAIS:3300 — Digital Product Management to complement her resume with a more visual and accessible professional presence. The site is fully responsive, accessibility-compliant, and requires no backend or build tools.

## Features

*Update this list to reflect what your project actually does.*

- Clean hero section with professional headshot, name, tagline, and call-to-action
- Detailed work experience section with role descriptions and real metrics
- Categorized skills displayed as styled pill badges
- Three featured project cards with tech stack labels and descriptions
- Sticky navigation bar with smooth-scroll anchor links
- Fully responsive layout — single-column on mobile, two-column on desktop
- WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility compliance throughout
- Functional contact links to email, LinkedIn, and GitHub

## Tech Stack

*Update the table to match your actual tech stack.*

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| HTML5 | Page structure and semantic markup |
| CSS3 | All styling, layout, and responsive design |
| JavaScript (vanilla) | Navigation active-state highlight on scroll |
| Google Fonts (Inter) | Typography |
| `serve` (npm) | Local static file server for development |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

*List everything a user needs installed before running the project locally.*

- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) (v18 or higher) — required to run the local server
- A modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Safari)
- A terminal / command line

### Installation

*Step-by-step instructions to get the project running locally.*

1. Clone the repository:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/bellaillingworth/personal-landing-page.git
   ```

2. Navigate into the project folder:
   ```bash
   cd personal-landing-page
   ```

3. Install the local server package:
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

4. Start the development server:
   ```bash
   npx serve . -l 5000
   ```

5. Open your browser and visit:
   ```
   http://localhost:5000
   ```

## Usage

*Describe how to use the project once it is running.*

Once the server is running, open `http://localhost:5000` in your browser to view the landing page. Use the sticky navigation bar to jump between sections: About, Experience, Skills, Projects, and Contact. All external links (LinkedIn, GitHub, email) are functional and open in a new tab where appropriate.

To customize the page with your own content, edit `index.html` directly — all text content lives there. To change colors, fonts, or layout, edit `css/stylesheet.css`. Add your own headshot by replacing `images/headshot.jpg` with your photo (keep the same filename).

## Project Structure

*Update file paths and comments to match your actual project.*

```
personal-landing-page/
├── index.html              # Main HTML file — all page content and structure
├── css/
│   └── stylesheet.css      # All styles — no inline CSS anywhere
├── js/
│   └── scripts.js          # Scroll-based nav highlighting
├── images/
│   └── headshot.jpg        # Professional headshot (local, not external URL)
├── PRD.md                  # Product requirements document
├── STANDARDS.md            # Technical and design standards for the project
├── package.json            # Node.js metadata and dependencies
└── README.md               # This file
```

## Changelog

*Update version numbers and dates as the project evolves.*

### v1.0.0 — March 22, 2026

- Initial release
- Hero section with headshot, name, tagline, and call-to-action button
- About section with bio, GPA, major, and graduation date
- Experience section covering ICAN Full-Stack App Developer role, Tippie Analytics Cooperative internship, and Etsy Poster Shop
- Skills section with three categorized groups and pill badge styling
- Projects section with three featured project cards (ICAN Mobile App, Real Estate Market Analysis, Movie Revenue Modeling)
- Contact section with email, LinkedIn, and GitHub links
- Sticky navigation with smooth-scroll anchor links
- Fully responsive layout from 320px and wider
- WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility compliance

## Known Issues / To-Do

*Replace with actual known gaps in your project.*

- [ ] No dark mode support — all styles are light-mode only
- [ ] Project cards do not link to live demos or GitHub repositories
- [ ] No favicon configured — browser tab shows a blank icon
- [ ] Color palette and typography values in `STANDARDS.md` still contain placeholder brackets that should be filled in

## Roadmap

*Describe what a future version of this project might include.*

- Add direct links from each project card to its GitHub repository or live demo
- Introduce a downloadable contact card or LinkedIn-style "connect" prompt in the contact section
- Add a favicon and Open Graph meta tags for better link previews when shared on LinkedIn or email
- Implement a lightweight CSS animation on the hero section for a more polished first impression
- Explore adding a filterable projects section as the portfolio grows beyond three entries

## Contributing

*Explain how someone else could contribute to your project.*

This is a personal portfolio project, but contributions such as bug reports, accessibility improvements, or design suggestions are welcome. To contribute:

1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2. Create a new branch for your change:
   ```bash
   git checkout -b your-feature-name
   ```
3. Make your edits to `index.html` or `css/stylesheet.css`
4. Commit your changes with a clear message:
   ```bash
   git commit -m "Describe what you changed"
   ```
5. Push the branch to your fork and open a pull request against `main`
